positions_held.brief_description_of_duties: Front-line customer service and repair order writing for a busy neighborhood automotive shop specializing in domestic and light-duty trucks. Responsible for greeting customers, listening carefully to their concerns, translating symptoms into clear repair descriptions for technicians, preparing detailed estimates, obtaining customer authorization, and keeping customers updated throughout the day. Managed daily scheduling for 4–5 technicians, prioritized work based on safety and customer needs, and coordinated parts ordering with multiple vendors to minimize downtime. Handled payment processing, warranty claims, and post-service follow-up calls to ensure customer satisfaction and encourage repeat business.

positions_held.position_s_held: Senior Service Advisor / Assistant Service Manager
positions_held.brief_description_of_duties: Promoted to oversee the service counter and assist the owner with day-to-day operations. Continued all standard service advisor duties while also training two junior advisors on systems, customer communication, and shop workflow. Implemented a more structured appointment scheduling process and a simple checklist for vehicle intake that reduced missed concerns and comebacks. Helped manage shop KPIs such as average repair order, technician productivity, and customer satisfaction scores. Took the lead on handling more complex customer situations, including second-opinion inspections, extended warranty approvals, and coordinating with insurance adjusters after accidents. Worked closely with technicians to explain recommended maintenance and safety-related repairs in plain language to customers. Assisted with inventory control for fast-moving parts, monitored returns and cores, and helped roll out a new shop management software system by creating quick-reference guides for other staff.

brief_description_of_duties.brief_description_of_duties:
As an Automotive Service Advisor, I worked directly with customers from the moment they walked in or called the shop. A typical day involved checking in 8–15 vehicles, asking detailed questions about symptoms (for example, when a customer mentioned a “rattling noise,” I would clarify whether it occurred during acceleration, braking, or over bumps), and documenting those concerns clearly on the repair order so the technicians had accurate starting points. I created preliminary estimates based on labor guides and parts pricing and then called customers to explain the recommended work, focusing on safety items first (brakes, steering, tires) and then preventive maintenance.

I also coordinated the workflow in the shop by assigning jobs to technicians according to their strengths (for instance, routing electrical diagnostics to the tech most experienced with wiring diagrams) and keeping an eye on promised completion times. Throughout the day, I updated customers if parts were delayed or additional issues were found, and I always tried to give realistic time frames rather than overpromising.

On the administrative side, I processed payments, applied coupons or loyalty credits, and made sure all documentation for warranties and returns was complete. I regularly followed up with customers a few days after major repairs to make sure everything was working properly, which helped us reduce complaints and build trust. I also helped maintain a clean and organized front counter and waiting area, restocking coffee and brochures and ensuring our posted shop policies were up to date and easy for customers to understand.

brief_description_of_duties.position_s_held: Senior Service Advisor / Assistant Service Manager
brief_description_of_duties.brief_description_of_duties:
In the Senior Service Advisor / Assistant Service Manager role, my responsibilities expanded beyond the front counter. I continued to handle complex repair orders and customer interactions, but I also helped shape how the entire service department operated. For example, I helped implement a standardized digital vehicle inspection process, working with technicians to create a checklist that included photos and short notes. This made it easier to show customers exactly what we were recommending and why, which significantly reduced misunderstandings about repair costs.

I was responsible for training and mentoring two newer advisors, walking them through how to prioritize jobs (e.g., a no-start vehicle blocking a bay versus a scheduled oil change) and how to have difficult conversations when a repair was more expensive than a customer expected. I often coached them on phrases that were honest but reassuring, like explaining that a brake fluid leak was a safety concern that couldn’t be postponed, but also offering options such as phased repairs or financing.

Operationally, I assisted the owner with reviewing weekly reports from our shop management software, including technician efficiency, comebacks, and parts margins. When we noticed repeat issues with certain vehicles (for example, recurring battery failures in specific models), I worked with the team to adjust our inspection procedures and proactively recommend testing. I also managed vendor relationships for key parts suppliers, negotiated delivery times for urgent jobs, and handled most of the escalated customer situations, such as warranty disputes or situations where a customer felt something was missed. In those cases, I would review the history, talk with the technician, and then present a fair solution to the customer, sometimes including goodwill discounts or partial labor coverage to keep the relationship positive.

supervisor_s_name.brief_description_of_duties: Front-line service advisor responsible for writing repair orders, coordinating with technicians, and communicating with customers about estimates, approvals, and completion times in a busy independent automotive repair shop.

supervisor_s_name.position_s_held: Senior Service Advisor / Assistant Service Manager
supervisor_s_name.brief_description_of_duties: Lead service advisor supporting daily operations of the service department, including staff training, workflow management, complex customer communication, and assisting the owner with performance metrics and vendor relationships.

employment_history.reason_for_leaving_if_applicable: I decided to leave West Ridge Automotive & Tire in order to relocate closer to family and to pursue a position with a larger organization that offers more structured opportunities for advancement and professional development. My spouse accepted a job in a neighboring city, and after discussing it with my supervisor several months in advance, we agreed on a transition plan so that I could help train my replacement and leave on good terms. There were no disciplinary issues or conflicts involved in my departure; it was purely a family and career growth decision, and my former supervisor has agreed to serve as a reference.

references.reference_3_occupation: Customer Service Supervisor, Northline Credit Union
